summary.textSample: Welcome to Sonata Asset Library!IntroSearchingSearch BarSectionsLabelsFiltersPinsVisual BrowsingBest PracticesDownloadingSingle Asset DownloadBulk DownloadResize & ConvertCroppingBest PracticesSharingShare LinksBest PracticesCommentingCommentsAnnotationsReply ThreadsBest PracticesAccessingPublic DAMPrivate AccessRequesting AccessHelpSearchingFinding the right assets quickly is essential for efficient workflow. The Sonata Asset Library offers multiple powerful search and discovery methods to help you locate exactly what you need. This chapter will guide you through each method and show you how to combine them for optimal results.Search Bar The search bar is your primary tool for finding assets. summary.title: How to Use Brandfolder | Downloading
summary.textSample: Welcome to Sonata Asset Library!IntroSearchingSearch BarSectionsLabelsFiltersPinsVisual BrowsingBest PracticesDownloadingSingle Asset DownloadBulk DownloadResize & ConvertCroppingBest PracticesSharingShare LinksBest PracticesCommentingCommentsAnnotationsReply ThreadsBest PracticesAccessingPublic DAMPrivate AccessRequesting AccessHelpDownloadingGetting your assets out of the system and into your projects is straightforward and flexible. Whether you need a single image for a quick email or hundreds of assets for a major campaign, the asset library provides several download options to meet your specific needs.Single Asset Download Download from Asset Card The most common way to download an individual asset is from within the asset's detailed card view, which provides full access to all download options. Select the type of collection you want to add to the collection section of your portal.
Select to add global or personal collections to your portal.
When you choose a featured collection, the first four assets in the collection display as previews.

Pin top collections you want others to see in a portal.

Click the Add icon to add more collections to a portal.
Remove a single collection from a portal by clicking the menu and deleting the card.
Click the trash can icon to delete a featured collection in a portal.

summary.textSample: Ask ChatGPTTutorialsDAM - Getting StartedOn this pageDAM - Getting StartedDAM Developer Tutorial​ In this tutorial, we'll guide you through the fundamental steps of interacting with the Aprimo Digital Asset Management (DAM) API. This straightforward approach is designed to help you quickly integrate with the API and perform essential operations such as authorization, searching for assets, downloading assets, retrieving metadata, uploading files, and creating new assets. You are encouraged to follow along using our provided DAM Developer Tutorial POSTMAN Collection. Rate LimitingBefore you begin developing, read about Aprimo Rate Limiting. Authorization​ Before you can interact with the Aprimo DAM API, you need to authenticate your application. Aprimo uses OAuth 2.0 for authorization, which involved the following steps: Registering a Client Registration Requesting an Access Token Includin

summary.title: DAM Rules | Aprimo Developer Documentation
summary.textSample: Ask ChatGPTWebhooksDAM RulesOn this pageDAM RulesDAM Rules enable developers to automate actions in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) platform based on defined conditions. These rules act as event-driven webhooks, designed to execute tasks like updating metadata, linking classifications, or initiating external processes when specific triggers occur. Rules are a powerful way to streamline workflows and enforce consistency in the DAM ecosystem. How DAM Rules Work​ DAM Rules operate using an If...Then logic. When a defined condition is met (e.g., a record is updated or a file is added), one or more actions are executed automatically. Rules can respond to a variety of triggers, such as: Object changes (e.g., a field is modified, or a record is reclassified). Specific states (e.g., a field contains a particular value). Scheduled events (e.g., daily checks for expired assets). Rules can be ap
